items.prompt: For a positive fixed-width unsigned integer x, what does x AND (x - 1) do?

options.text: It clears the lowest set bit and preserves all higher bits.
It keeps only the lowest set bit.
It sets the lowest clear bit.
It shifts every bit right by one.

feedback.reason: Subtracting one flips the lowest set bit to zero and turns lower zeros into ones.
feedback.details: AND with the original x removes that one while leaving higher bits unchanged.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.isolate_lowest_one: That is x AND -x under two's-complement arithmetic.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.set_lowest_zero: The operation removes a set bit.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.shift_right: Higher bit positions remain in place.

items.prompt: Under a fixed-width two's-complement model, what does x AND -x isolate for nonzero x?

options.text: A one-hot mask containing only x's lowest set bit.
Only the highest set bit.
Every set bit of x.
Always zero.

feedback.reason: Two's-complement negation preserves the lowest one position in both x and -x.
feedback.details: AND leaves exactly that shared lowest set bit.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.highest_one: Negation does not preserve only the most significant one.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_ones: Higher set bits do not all survive the AND.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero: A nonzero fixed-width pattern has a lowest set bit.

constraints: Use one fixed width and two's-complement negation.

items.prompt: What are 0 AND (0 - 1) and 0 AND -0 in a fixed-width unsigned/two's-complement bit model?

options.text: Both are zero.
The first result is all ones.
The second result is all ones.
Both are necessarily undefined.

feedback.reason: Zero contains no set bit to remove or isolate.
feedback.details: Both AND expressions therefore produce zero.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.first_all_ones: Although 0 - 1 wraps to all ones in unsigned arithmetic, AND with zero is zero.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.undefined_both: The question supplies a fixed-width bit model.

constraints: Use fixed-width unsigned subtraction for 0 - 1 and fixed-width two's-complement negation.

items.prompt: What is 10110000 AND (10110000 - 1) in 8-bit unsigned arithmetic?

feedback.reason: The lowest set bit is at position 4.
feedback.details: Subtracting one gives 10101111, and AND clears position 4 while preserving the higher set bits.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_00010000: That isolates the lowest set bit rather than clearing it.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_10101111: That is x - 1 before the AND.

items.prompt: What is 10110000 AND -10110000 under 8-bit two's-complement arithmetic?

feedback.reason: The lowest set position of 10110000 is bit 4.
feedback.details: The x AND -x identity produces the one-hot mask 00010000.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_10100000: That is the lowest-set-bit removal result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_11110000: The isolated result must be one-hot.

constraints: Use exactly 8 bits and two's-complement negation.

items.prompt: How many iterations does a loop perform if it repeatedly executes x = x AND (x - 1) until x becomes zero?

options.text: Exactly the number of set bits initially present in x.
Always the full word width.
Exactly x iterations.
Always one iteration.

feedback.reason: Each iteration removes exactly one set bit.
feedback.details: The loop terminates after every initial one has been cleared.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bit_width: Each iteration removes a one, not necessarily one bit position.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.numeric_value: The numeric value can be much larger than its popcount.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one: Values with multiple set bits require multiple removals.

items.prompt: If lowbit = x AND -x equals 00100000, what is the zero-based position of x's lowest set bit?

feedback.details: The one-hot mask directly identifies the lowest set-bit position.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.position_32: 32 is the place value, not the zero-based position.

items.prompt: Which expression correctly tests whether a positive unsigned x is a power of two?

options.text: x is nonzero and (x AND (x - 1)) equals zero.
(x AND (x - 1)) is nonzero.
(x AND -x) equals zero.
x XOR 1 equals zero.

feedback.reason: A positive power of two contains exactly one set bit.
feedback.details: Removing its lowest set bit leaves zero; the explicit nonzero check excludes x = 0.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.clear_nonzero: That means x has at least two set bits.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.lowbit_zero: A nonzero x always has a nonzero lowbit.

items.prompt: Why should a repeated x = x AND (x - 1) bit-count loop use an unsigned or explicitly fixed-width model?

options.text: Subtraction, negative patterns, and termination should not depend on unspecified signed overflow or width behavior.
Unsigned values are always asymptotically faster.
Signed values have no bit representation.
Unsigned input is required for sorting.

feedback.reason: Bit identities are statements about a declared finite bit pattern.
feedback.details: Using unsigned or explicit fixed-width semantics makes wraparound and every intermediate value reviewable.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.unsigned_faster: The issue is semantic definition, not asymptotic speed.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.signed_no_bits: They do, but language rules may differ.

items.prompt: Which statements about x AND (x - 1) are correct for nonzero fixed-width unsigned x? Select all that apply.

options.text: It removes exactly one set bit.
All bit positions above the removed one keep their previous values.
All positions below the removed bit are zero in the final result.
It returns only the removed one bit.

feedback.reason: The operation clears the lowest one without moving or changing higher positions.
feedback.details: It is a removal identity, not an isolation identity.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.higher_preserved: Subtract-one changes only at and below the lowest one.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.lower_zero: Those positions were zero in x and AND keeps them zero.

items.prompt: Which statements about x AND -x are correct under fixed-width two's-complement arithmetic? Select all that apply.

options.text: For nonzero x, the result is a one-hot mask.
For x = 0, the result is 0.
The unsigned result equals the place value of the lowest set bit.
The result is x with its lowest set bit removed.

feedback.reason: The lowbit identity isolates one position rather than returning the remainder of x.
feedback.details: Its signed numeric interpretation may be awkward at the sign position, so the bit-pattern contract should remain explicit.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.place_value: A one-hot bit at k has value 2^k.

constraints: Use one fixed width and two's-complement negation.

items.prompt: Which tests are important for lowest-set-bit logic? Select all that apply.

options.text: x has exactly one set bit.
Only the most significant bit is set in the fixed-width word.
Only one value with many set bits.

feedback.reason: Zero, one-hot, and sign-position cases expose different model errors.
feedback.details: Dense patterns are useful but not sufficient by themselves.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_many_ones: This misses the critical zero and one-hot boundaries.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.power_two: Removal should yield zero and isolation should return x.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.lowbit_sign_position: It exercises width and signed-interpretation assumptions.

items.prompt: Arrange one iteration of Brian Kernighan's set-bit counting loop.

feedback.reason: Each iteration corresponds to exactly one removed set bit.
feedback.details: The counter therefore equals the original popcount at termination.

items.prompt: Arrange the reasoning that x AND -x isolates the lowest set bit.

elements.text: Write x as a higher prefix, then its lowest one, then only zeros below.
Form -x by complementing and adding one within the fixed width.
Observe that x and -x share the lowest one but differ above it.
AND the patterns to keep only that shared one.

feedback.reason: The proof depends on the carry behavior of two's-complement negation.
feedback.details: Width must be fixed so complement and addition are defined.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for extracting every set-bit one-hot mask from x.

elements.text: Use or record that one-hot mask.

feedback.reason: Isolation identifies the next bit, and removal guarantees progress.
feedback.details: The sequence emits set bits from least significant to most significant.

items.prompt: A fixed-width unsigned value contains p set bits. Brian Kernighan's loop counts them using x = x AND (x - 1). Select time and auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: The loop stops when all p set bits are removed.

feedback.reason: The loop iterates once per set bit rather than once per word position.
feedback.details: This can be faster than an O(w) full-width scan for sparse values.

items.prompt: A straightforward algorithm inspects all w positions of an arbitrary-width integer to count set bits. Select time and auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: Every bit position is examined exactly once.

feedback.reason: The algorithm pays for every represented position, including zeros.
feedback.details: This contrasts with the O(popcount) removal loop under fixed-word operations.

items.prompt: A fixed-width value has p set bits, and an algorithm isolates and outputs one one-hot mask per set bit. Select time, auxiliary space excluding output, and output space.
constraints: The p masks are materialized in a result list.

feedback.reason: The algorithm performs one constant-time step per emitted set bit.
feedback.details: The materialized one-hot masks are required output, not auxiliary state.
